tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
New York City Police Department (NYPD)
Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards Select Bus Service

payload.project_name: Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards Select Bus Service
payload.description: Third public meeting for the Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards SBS project took place November 5, 2014. Three design concepts presented: offset bus lanes, main road bus lanes, and median bus lanes.

tool_args.label: Q52 LTD

payload.description: Existing limited-stop bus route along Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards corridor, running from Woodside to Arverne.

tool_args.label: Q53 LTD

payload.description: Existing limited-stop bus route along Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards corridor, running from Rego Park to Breezy Point. One-way travel times can vary by up to 30 minutes (from 55 to 85 minutes).

tool_args.label: Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards

payload.description: Corridor heavily used by bus riders, drivers, and pedestrians. Roughly 400,000 residents live within a 15-minute walk. 10 bus routes operate along the corridor. Connects to A train at Liberty Av, J/Z at Jamaica, and M/R at Queens Blvd.

tool_args.label: Design Concept One – Offset Bus Lanes

payload.description: Bus lane located one lane away from curb. Preserves curbside parking and loading access. Maintains three general traffic lanes in each direction. Service roads dedicated to local access, parking, and buses only. Includes bus bulbs at SBS stations, medians and neckdowns throughout corridor.

tool_args.label: Design Concept Two – Main Road Bus Lanes

payload.description: Curbside bus lanes within the main road with boarding at expanded service road medians. Service roads created between Eliot Av and Park Lane South where they do not currently exist. Three traffic lanes maintained: two thru lanes in main road, one lane in service road. South of Rockaway Blvd/Liberty Av, offset bus lane configuration used.

tool_args.label: Design Concept Three – Median Bus Lanes

payload.description: Median bus lanes allow buses to operate without curbside conflicts. SBS and local bus stations on widened center medians with shelters, plantings, and waiting space. Passing lanes at station locations along Woodhaven Blvd for SBS to bypass local buses. No passing lanes on Cross Bay Blvd. Service roads removed where they exist. Parking restrictions at select locations during rush hour.

tool_args.label: Third Public Meeting – Woodhaven/Cross Bay SBS

payload.description: Third public meeting for the Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards Select Bus Service project at Living Word Christian Fellowship in Ozone Park, Queens. Open-house style with 6 stations, 96 attendees.

tool_args.label: Select design direction and develop details

payload.description: Select a design direction and develop details for the preferred design with continued community input.

tool_args.label: Implement Select Bus Service – 2017

payload.description: Implement Select Bus Service on Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards.

SBS is a partnership between NYC DOT and MTA

payload.claim_text: The Select Bus Service (SBS) program is a partnership between the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) and the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA).
payload.subject: Select Bus Service program

tool_args.label: SBS features: dedicated bus lanes, off-board fare payment, TSP, enhanced stations, real-time info

payload.claim_text: SBS includes features like dedicated bus lanes, off-board fare payment, transit signal priority, enhanced stations, and real-time passenger information to make bus trips faster and more comfortable.
payload.subject: Select Bus Service features

tool_args.label: Community outreach includes CAC, public workshops, community board presentations

payload.claim_text: Community outreach for the Woodhaven/Cross Bay SBS project includes: 1) a Community Advisory Committee, which consists of elected officials, community boards, representatives from civic organizations, and bus riders; 2) Public Workshops and Open Houses; and 3) Community Board and Stakeholder Presentations.
payload.subject: Woodhaven/Cross Bay SBS community outreach

tool_args.label: Community concerns: bus service/reliability, congestion, safety, unaccommodating streets

payload.claim_text: Through the outreach process, the community identified several major points of concern, including: bus service and reliability, congestion, safety, and streets that are unaccommodating to all users.
payload.subject: Woodhaven/Cross Bay corridor community concerns

tool_args.label: SBS off-board fare collection process

payload.claim_text: Before boarding the bus, customers must pay their fares at a Select Bus Service station using the MetroCard or coin machine where they will receive a ticket as proof of payment. When the bus arrives, customers may enter or exit through any of the bus doors. Riders without a ticket will be subject to a $100 fare evasion summons.
payload.subject: SBS fare collection

tool_args.label: NYCDOT and MTA planning to apply for Federal Grant

payload.claim_text: NYCDOT and the MTA are planning to apply for a Federal Grant in order to fund the capital costs of the project. Once the project is built, NYCDOT will be responsible for maintaining the road.
payload.subject: Woodhaven/Cross Bay SBS project funding
